title: alpinia calcarata
intro: Alpinia calcarata, also known as the bitter ginger, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Zingiberaceae family. It is native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. Alpinia calcarata has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant's roots are used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues.
